sourceMovie.title: The Legend of Drunken Master
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Legend of Drunken Master (1994) stuck because it commits to its action premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Lau Kar-Leung's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The action and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Drunken Master
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Legend of Drunken Master clicked for you, Drunken Master (1978) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Wendy Wu: Homecoming Warrior
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Legend of Drunken Master clicked for you, Wendy Wu: Homecoming Warrior (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Legend of Drunken Master?
faq.answer: Try Drunken Master, Wendy Wu: Homecoming Warrior, Snake in the Eagle's Shadow—each shares DNA with The Legend of Drunken Master while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Legend of Drunken Master?
faq.answer: The Legend of Drunken Master (1994) is best described as Action, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Legend of Drunken Master part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Drunken Master Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
